Find Your Chance
Use one of our programs to develop yourself. Be the part of the big network with COPERNICUS BERLIN!
EXCELENCE SCHOLARSHIP
PROGRAM IES
Scholarships of COPERNICUS BERLIN offer a unique opportunity for personal and professional development in a multicultural area. Through intensive exchange, the scholarship holders create a common Europe.
Learn more
INTERNSHIP PROGRAM
LEADERS FOR FUTURE (LF)
The internship program creates leaders for the future. Through these career opportunities, you can unleash your potential as well as develop your professional knowledge and skills. An important part is also building a professional network.
Learn more
ERASMUS+
TRAINING COURSES
The Erasmus+ projects connect the borders of Europe, link people of all generations and create common values. Seminars on various topics develop personal and professional skills as well as knowledge of the participants.
Learn more

documents.title: About Copernicus Berlin and Our Mission in Education and Exchange
documents.text:
About Copernicus Berlin: Scholarships, Erasmus+ Projects, and Youth Empowerment
Who Are We?
Copernicus Berlin e.V. is a Berlin-based nonprofit organization that creates scholarships, European and international projects, training courses, internships, and international exchange opportunities for young people, students, youth workers, and civil society actors. Since 2000, we have worked to expand access to education, strengthen intercultural dialogue, and connect people across Europe and beyond. Our work combines formal and non-formal education, international mobility, digital innovation, and social impact.
Why Are We Called Copernicus?
Copernicus Berlin takes its name from Nicolaus Copernicus, the 16th-century scientist whose heliocentric worldview fundamentally changed the way humanity understood the universe. His discovery did more than reshape astronomy — it became a symbol of intellectual courage, new perspectives, and transformative thought.
The idea behind Copernicus Berlin began in 1995, while the association was officially established in 2000. Since then, this spirit has remained at the heart of our work and identity. Visit our
history
page to discover our journey, milestones, and the development of Copernicus Berlin over the years.
For us, the name Copernicus represents the power of education, reflection, and international exchange to shift perspectives and create positive change. Just as Copernicus redefined how people saw the world, we believe that learning and cooperation can help individuals and communities rethink possibilities, overcome boundaries, and move toward a more sustainable, inclusive, and forward-looking future.
The heliocentric idea also resonates with our internal philosophy. Within Copernicus Berlin, every individual plays an essential role in a democratically structured and collaborative community. Each person contributes independently, yet remains connected to a shared purpose and common vision.
In this way, our name expresses both our inspiration and our identity: openness to new ideas, respect for every individual, and commitment to a better future through education, cooperation, and social engagement.
Structure of Our Organisation
Copernicus Berlin is a democratically structured nonprofit association built on cooperation, shared responsibility, and active participation. Our organization brings together a diverse, international, and bright team of people who contribute with different backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and areas of expertise. This diversity is one of our greatest strengths and plays an essential role in shaping our work, our atmosphere, and our vision.
Our team members are united by a shared commitment to education, international cooperation, social engagement, and positive change. By bringing together people from different cultural, academic, and professional contexts, we create an environment in which ideas can develop through dialogue, collaboration, and mutual learning.
The structure of our organization is designed to combine clear coordination with collaborative and democratic processes. This allows us to work effectively while remaining open, people-centered, and participatory. Different team members take on different roles and responsibilities in the development, implementation, and support of our projects, activities, and partnerships, always working toward common goals and shared values.
At the heart of Copernicus Berlin is the belief that every individual has an important role to play. We value initiative, responsibility, creativity, and the unique contribution of each team member. In this way, our organizational structure is not only functional, but also reflects who we are as a community: international, inclusive, dynamic, and united by a common purpose.
To learn more about the people behind Copernicus Berlin, visit our
Our Team
page.
Values That We Share
Our work is guided by three core values: European, Network, and Progress.
We Share European Values
At Copernicus Berlin, European values form an essential foundation of our identity and our work. We believe in a Europe built on peace, cooperation, solidarity, democracy, human dignity, and mutual respect. For us, being European is not only a geographical idea, but also a commitment to openness, dialogue, inclusion, and shared responsibility.
These values are reflected in the way we create educational opportunities, build international partnerships, and connect people from different countries, cultures, and backgrounds. We see intercultural exchange and cross-border cooperation as important tools for strengthening understanding, reducing prejudice, and building a more connected and resilient society.
We Share the Value of Network
Network is one of the central values of Copernicus Berlin because we believe that meaningful change happens through connection, cooperation, and exchange. We bring together individuals, organizations, institutions, and communities in order to create opportunities for learning, partnership, and joint development.
For us, a network is more than a group of contacts. It is a living community built on trust, communication, shared goals, and mutual support. Through our local and international networks, we create spaces where ideas can grow, knowledge can be shared, and new initiatives can emerge. This value is visible in the way we work with partners across Europe and beyond, and in the way we connect participants to wider communities and opportunities.
We Share the Value of Progress
Progress reflects our belief in development, innovation, and the continuous search for better ways of learning, cooperating, and creating impact. At Copernicus Berlin, we believe that progress begins with curiosity, openness to change, and the courage to move beyond familiar boundaries.
This value guides us in supporting personal growth, educational advancement, social innovation, and international cooperation. We aim to create opportunities that help people strengthen their knowledge, discover new perspectives, and actively shape their future. For us, progress is not only about moving forward, but about moving forward with purpose, responsibility, and a positive vision for society.
Who Are Our Partners?
Copernicus Berlin works with a broad network of partners in Germany, across Europe, and beyond. Our partners include non-governmental organizations, municipalities, educational institutions, public bodies, youth organizations, experts, and civil society actors who share our commitment to education, international cooperation, inclusion, and positive social change.
Over the years, we have built partnerships with organizations and institutions from different countries and sectors in order to develop scholarships, exchanges, training courses, youth projects, mobility activities, and other educational and social initiatives. These partnerships allow us to combine expertise, connect communities, and create opportunities that are international, practical, and impactful.

What We Do?
At Copernicus Berlin, we create educational, professional, and international opportunities that help young people grow, connect, and contribute to society. Our work brings together formal education, non-formal learning, international mobility, and practical experience in order to support personal development, active citizenship, and long-term social impact.
We design and implement scholarship programmes, youth exchanges, training courses, internships, and international cooperation projects that open doors for students, young professionals, youth workers, and civil society actors from diverse backgrounds. Through our initiatives, participants gain knowledge, practical skills, intercultural experience, and access to strong international networks.
A key part of our work is the development of educational pathways that combine academic learning with real-life experience. We support participants through study opportunities, professional placements, volunteering, mentoring, and project-based learning that strengthen both personal and professional capacities.
We are also actively involved in Erasmus+ and other international cooperation frameworks, through which we organise mobility projects, trainings, partnership initiatives, and capacity-building activities across Europe and beyond. Our projects address important themes such as youth empowerment, inclusion, digital skills, media literacy, civic participation, intercultural dialogue, and social innovation.
In addition, we work with partners including NGOs, institutions, municipalities, educators, and community actors to create meaningful collaborations and sustainable impact. By combining innovation, international cooperation, and a strong educational mission, we aim to make learning more accessible, practical, and transformative for the people and communities we serve.
Our Mission
We believe that education should not only provide knowledge, but also open pathways to personal development, intercultural understanding, active citizenship, and professional growth.
Our work is guided by the conviction that international exchange, learning, and cooperation can bring people closer, reduce barriers, and strengthen more inclusive and resilient communities. For this reason, we develop programmes and initiatives that support access to education, mobility, dialogue, practical experience, and social engagement for young people and diverse communities.
We are committed to building bridges between people, cultures, and ideas. Through scholarships, training opportunities, exchanges, internships, and international projects, we aim to support individuals in discovering their potential, broadening their perspectives, and becoming active actors of positive change.
At the same time, our mission extends beyond individual development. We seek to contribute to a more connected, equitable, and forward-looking society by promoting inclusion, participation, cooperation, and innovation in education and civil society. In everything we do, we strive to create opportunities that are not only accessible and impactful, but also meaningful for both participants and the communities around them.
Our Main Areas of Work
Our work is rooted in a broad and interdisciplinary range of expertise that responds to the needs of young people, educators, youth workers, institutions, and communities. We develop projects, educational activities, and international cooperation initiatives in areas that strengthen both individual capacities and wider social resilience.
Our main areas of work include youth participation, democracy, and civic life, where we support active citizenship, democratic engagement, and meaningful involvement of young people in society. We are also strongly engaged in media literacy, information integrity, and digital citizenship, helping participants critically understand information, navigate online spaces responsibly, and respond to misinformation and disinformation. These fields are closely connected to our work on digital skills, tech empowerment, and innovation, where we promote practical digital competences and the confident, ethical use of new technologies.
Another important pillar of our work is inclusion, diversity, equality, and anti-discrimination. We create opportunities that bring together people from different social, cultural, and educational backgrounds and aim to make participation more accessible and fair. In parallel, we work in the fields of migration, integration, and intercultural communities, as well as peacebuilding, conflict transformation, and social cohesion, reflecting our long-standing commitment to dialogue between people, countries, and cultures.
Education remains at the heart of our mission. For this reason, we focus strongly on education support, learning pathways, and non-formal education, alongside youth leadership, mentoring, and peer approaches. Through scholarships, trainings, exchanges, and practical learning experiences, we help participants build knowledge, confidence, and long-term perspectives. We also support employability, entrepreneurship, and economic empowerment, enabling young people and professionals to strengthen their career prospects and social impact.
In addition, Copernicus Berlin works on themes such as youth mental health, wellbeing, and healthy lifestyles, climate, sustainability, and green transition, arts, culture, creativity, and heritage, communication, outreach, and visibility, and project management, grant work, and organizational capacity. This wide spectrum allows us to design holistic initiatives that combine education, innovation, international cooperation, and community development.
To learn more about the topics we work on and the fields in which we have developed experience, visit our
Expertise
page.
Why Copernicus Berlin Is Different?
Copernicus Berlin stands out through its combination of educational mission, international perspective, practical opportunities, and strong commitment to personal development. We do not see education only as the transfer of knowledge, but as a transformative process that connects learning, experience, responsibility, and real human interaction.
What makes our approach unique is the way we combine formal education, non-formal learning, international mobility, professional experience, and community engagement within one ecosystem of opportunities. Participants are not only invited to study or attend a programme, but to become part of a broader environment that encourages growth, initiative, intercultural understanding, and active contribution.
We also place strong emphasis on creating opportunities that are both international and personal. Our programmes connect people from different countries, backgrounds, and disciplines, while at the same time paying close attention to individual journeys, needs, and potential. This balance allows us to build meaningful educational experiences that are not only globally oriented, but also deeply human.
Another distinctive element of Copernicus Berlin is our belief in combining innovation with accessibility. We work with modern tools, digital solutions, and forward-looking approaches, while keeping our mission centred on inclusion, empowerment, and social value. We aim to make international and educational opportunities more understandable, reachable, and impactful for a wide range of participants.
Our work is also shaped by long-term vision and strong partnerships. Through cooperation with organizations, institutions, educators, and communities across Europe and beyond, we create programmes that are relevant, dynamic, and rooted in real needs. This allows us to go beyond one-time activities and contribute to sustainable learning, international cooperation, and positive social change.
In essence, Copernicus Berlin is different because we create more than programmes. We create pathways, environments, and connections that help people learn, grow, and engage with the world in a meaningful way.

documents.title: Meet the Copernicus Berlin Team and Core Contributors
documents.text:
About us
Meet our international team
Our team is one of our greatest strengths. We are a diverse, inclusive, international, and bright community of people who bring together different backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and areas of expertise. What unites us is a shared commitment to education, international cooperation, social engagement, and positive change.
Executive Team
The Executive Team is responsible for the strategic direction, coordination, and day-to-day management of Copernicus Berlin. Its members take leading roles in developing projects, building partnerships, overseeing operations, and ensuring that the organization works in line with its mission and values. The Executive Team helps turn ideas into action and provides the structure needed for sustainable and effective work.
Davit Budaghyan
Managing Director

Aleksandr Rasskazchikov

documents.title: Cobi Studio: Creative Projects and Educational Media
documents.text:
About us
CoBi Studio
creative division
We are a dynamic and innovative hub for all things creative! Our passion is to transform imagination into reality through art, design, and innovation. At our creative studio, we bring dreams to life, one project at a time.
Imagination Meets Innovation
In a world where attention spans are fleeting and information overload is the norm, the ability to convey a powerful message is more critical than ever. At CoBi Studio, we explore the incredible potential of visual material to communicate messages effectively and leave a lasting impact.
Our Approach
At CoBi Studio, we are passionate about harnessing the potential of visual material to deliver a message with impact. Our team of skilled designers, illustrators, and visual storytellers work collaboratively to:
Design Stunning Graphics:
From captivating images to striking infographics, we create visually compelling content that grabs audience's attention.
Craft Engaging Videos:
Our video production experts bring a narrative to life through creative and informative videos that resonate with viewers.
Illustrate Story:
Illustrations and animations are used to simplify complex concepts and transform them into engaging narratives.
Tailor-Made Solutions:
We understand that every message is unique. That's why we customize our visual material to align with brand and message.
Why Visual Material Matters
In today's fast-paced digital age, words alone often fall short in capturing the attention and engagement of an audience. Visual material, on the other hand, has the unique power to:
Capture Attention:
Eye-catching visuals immediately draw viewers in and pique their curiosity, making them more receptive to your message.
Simplify Complexity:
Complex ideas and data can be simplified and made more accessible through infographics, charts, and visual storytelling.
Evoke Emotions:
Images, videos, and graphics have the ability to evoke powerful emotions, fostering a deeper connection between the message and audience.
Cross Cultural Barriers:
Visuals transcend language barriers, enabling to reach a global audience with ease.

documents.title: Scholarship Opportunities at Copernicus Berlin
documents.text:
Scholarships
Our scholarship programs
Since 2000, COPERNICUS BERLIN has proudly granted over 1000 scholarships to individuals from 105 countries. Our support has empowered students, graduates, and professionals worldwide to pursue their dreams and contribute positively to our global community.
IES Program
International Excellence Scholarship provides opportunity for students from post-sovetic countries to complete exchange semester and volunteer in Berlin
Read more
>
Leaders for Future
Professional development scholarship which involves volunteering in Berlin and participation in workshops
Read more
>
Professionals in residence
Extended track for former IES scholarship holders that builds long-term professional competencies through applied practice.
Read more
>
Ukrainian Talent
Scholarship program for ambitious Ukrainians who want to study, grow professionally, and join an international community in Berlin.
Read more
>
Scholarships idea
In numerous nations, young individuals face hurdles in accessing top-tier university education, limited chances for international study experiences, and minimal opportunities to apply their theoretical knowledge practically. This education deficit contributes to democratic deficits, weak socio-economic conditions, and social inequality.
Since the year 2000, COPERNICUS BERLIN has been dedicated to leveling the playing field in education. Our mission is to empower young minds with equal educational opportunities, ultimately fostering the growth of democracy, economic prosperity, and progressive civil societies. We believe in equipping the youth with unique experiences that they can later apply in their home countries, driving positive change.
COPERNICUS BERLIN is committed to fostering a new generation of professionals who not only excel in their fields but also contribute positively to their home countries and the global community. Through our internship initiatives, we aim to empower young talents with the skills and confidence they need to succeed, while simultaneously promoting international cooperation and understanding.

documents.title: IES Program at Copernicus Berlin
documents.text:
Scholarships
International Excellence Scholarship Program (IES)
Experience of IES Scholarship Holders
IES offers motivated students from Eastern and Southeastern Europe, the Caucasus, and Central Asia a great opportunity to empower themselves through meaningful volunteering at one of Germany’s oldest and largest youth-working NGOs, while studying at top universities in Berlin.
You will gain real experience, contribute to important projects, build new skills, and study at leading institutions such as HU Berlin , FU Berlin, HWR Berlin, University of Potsdam .
IES Program
The scholarship program in Berlin offers much more than studying at a good university. It gives you the chance to empower yourself through real volunteering in one of the oldest and largest youth-working NGOs in Germany. By joining our daily work, you support projects, help at events, assist international activities, and contribute to the growth of our community.
Volunteering lets you experience German culture from the inside. You take part in cultural events, community activities, and teamwork that helps you build friendships and truly feel part of Berlin. Learning happens not only in the classroom but also through responsibility, cooperation, and real impact.
Our program is not passive. Every scholarship holder shapes their own path. Your ideas and energy influence the projects you join and the experience you build. We encourage you to be active, creative, and involved.
To make your start easy, our team guides you through the transition to Berlin. Mentors support you in the first weeks, give practical advice, explain the association, and help you settle into your new life.
In our Scholarship Program, volunteering stands at the center. It strengthens your studies, connects you with a diverse community, and helps you grow personally and professionally while discovering Berlin in a meaningful way.
Check the statistics
Explore data’s fascinating world and see how it drives our success, reflecting our commitment to excellence.

Internship
After passing exams you will start a full time internship to implement your theoretical knowledge in practice and grow professionally.
Travel
You will have an opportunity to travel to Germany and beyond to experience European culture and languages.

A full scholarship of the IES program for one person amounts to Approx. 12,590 EUR and consists of the following parts

Application documents

Onboarding & Online volunteering

APPLY NOW
FAQ
What will be my volunteering activities?
Your volunteering activities will focus on supporting the work of Copernicus Berlin and contributing to the growth of our community. As an NGO, Copernicus Berlin is built and sustained by the active involvement of its members, so every scholarship holder plays an important role. Volunteering tasks may include but not limit on: Preparing social media content and helping with communication materials Supporting the development of our website and digital tools (if you have technical skills) Assisting in the preparation and organisation of educational training courses Helping with administrative, creative, or research tasks, depending on your interests and abilities We encourage you to contribute in the areas where you feel most comfortable and where you can bring the most value. However, sometimes you will also need to complete assigned tasks, especially when they are important for ongoing projects or organisational needs. Overall, most of your volunteering experience will be meaningful, flexible, and tailored to both your strengths and the needs of the organisation.
How much time will volunteering occupy per week?
There is no fixed amount of weekly volunteering hours. It depends entirely on your personal motivation and the tasks you choose to work on. At Copernicus Berlin we encourage our scholarship holders to develop their skills by working on real tasks and creating real impact. We are always happy when someone explores new areas, takes initiative, or suggests improvements. Your volunteering experience can be as intensive and as enriching as you choose to make it.
Can I choose my volunteering tasks?
Yes, of course. You are free to choose the volunteering tasks that match your interests, skills, and motivation. We will be very glad if you take a proactive approach and suggest improvements or new ideas that can help Copernicus Berlin grow and develop. Your initiative is always welcome. At the same time, sometimes you will be assigned specific tasks that are important for ongoing projects, and you will still need to complete them.
Are volunteering tasks mandatory?
Yes. Volunteering is an essential part of the IES Scholarship and a core component of your participation. While you have freedom to choose many of your tasks and contribute in areas you enjoy, completing volunteering responsibilities is mandatory for all scholarship holders. Copernicus Berlin is an NGO, and we are happy to give you the opportunity to come to Berlin, study, and develop your skills. At the same time, we also expect meaningful contributions from your side to support the organisation and its community.
Will I volunteer individually or in a team?
It depends on the task. Some activities you can complete individually, while others are done together with our team. Copernicus Berlin has a group of young, motivated, and creative people with whom you will work throughout the preparation process and later in Berlin. You will definitely meet them, collaborate, and become part of the community.
Will my volunteering tasks change throughout the semester?
Yes. Your volunteering tasks can change depending on the period and the needs of the organisation.
Will I have a volunteering supervisor?
Yes. You will have a dedicated supervisor from our team. Before you arrive in Berlin, you will regularly join online calls to discuss your tasks, track your progress, and prepare for the program. After you come to Berlin, these meetings continue offline. During these meetings we assign new tasks, discuss how the week went, and plan the upcoming activities.
How is my volunteering performance evaluated?
During the scholarship we use an evaluation system that helps you understand your progress and development. You will receive points and grades in different areas, which allows you to see your strengths and understand where you can grow or improve. It is important to note that in cases of persistent stagnation or a continuous decrease in grades, the scholarship may be revoked. The goal of the grading system is there to support your growth and help you stay active and motivated throughout the program.
Are there any free days?
Most volunteering tasks are project based, which means you work toward completing a specific task or producing a result by the deadline. Because of this you can usually arrange your time freely and decide when to work on your tasks. However, please note that during some periods of the program there will be mandatory meetings, activities, and training courses. On those days your presence is required. Outside of these scheduled activities, your time is flexible.
Do I get a reference letter for volunteering?
Yes. A reference letter can be issued upon request, confirming your contribution and the tasks you completed during the scholarship.
What happens if I miss a deadline or cannot complete tasks?
We always try to assign realistic deadlines and tasks that are fully achievable within the given timeframe. Most tasks are selected based on your skills and background from your CV, video interview, and the information you provided during the application process. We do not give tasks that we believe you cannot complete. If you feel that you cannot finish a task on time, the most important thing is to inform your supervisor in advance. We can discuss the situation, adjust the deadline if needed, or help you find a solution. However, if we see a consistent mismatch between the skills you demonstrated in your application and your actual performance, or if you repeatedly fail to complete tasks without proper communication, we reserve the right to revoke the scholarship. This is a rare situation and happens only when absolutely necessary. The goal is always to support you.
Can I travel to other European countries during the IES program?
Yes, but only if one of the supervisors accompanies you and the trip is approved in advance. Without a supervisor, travelling outside of Germany is very risky and therefore not allowed within the IES programme. It can lead to problems with your visa/residence status, health and travel insurance, and your scholarship obligations.
Can I travel around Germany?
Yes, you can travel within Germany, but the trip must be approved in advance. Please request approval not earlier than 2 weeks before your planned travel and not later than 1 week before it. We need this time to adjust the schedule, coordinate tasks, or reassign your volunteering responsibilities if necessary.
Can I travel for emergencies?
Yes, but only in exceptional cases. If an emergency occurs, we will ask you to provide proof so that we can document the situation and adjust your schedule accordingly. Your wellbeing and safety are important to us, and we will support you in genuine emergency situations.
Does Copernicus cover any travel costs?
For personal travel, no. These trips are fully your responsibility. However, if you travel to participate in a Copernicus Berlin event, then yes. During such official trips, your accommodation, food, and travel expenses are usually covered by Copernicus Berlin.
Can I travel during holidays?
During the first 6 months of the IES programme, travelling back to your home country for holiday or leisure reasons is not permitted. Holiday travel is not considered an emergency and therefore does not qualify as a valid reason for leaving Germany in this period.
Can I stay extra time in Germany after the scholarship ends?
It depends on your visa type and your individual situation. After the scholarship ends, Copernicus Berlin does not provide any benefits, support, or financial coverage, so whether you can remain in Germany depends entirely on your residence permit and legal requirements. However, in some cases it is possible to extend the scholarship program itself. Extensions are not guaranteed and depend on your performance, motivation, and the availability of places, but in certain situations they can be considered.
Do I get discounted public transportation?
Yes. While you are enrolled at a Berlin university, you receive the Deutschlandsemesterticket , which gives you free access to public transportation across all of Germany.
Will I live alone or with another student?
Most of the Copernicus Berlin apartments are shared. You will live in a room with no more than one other scholarship holder. The shared setup helps create a supportive atmosphere and allows you to connect with other participants more easily.
Is the accommodation shared or private?
The accommodation is reserved exclusively for Copernicus Berlin scholarship holders. You will live together with other participants of the program, not with external tenants.
Are utilities included in the accommodation?
Yes. All utilities are included, such as electricity and internet. You will not need to pay anything extra for basic living costs.
Are guests allowed?
No. Guests from outside the program are not allowed in the accommodation. Only other Copernicus Berlin scholarship holders and members of the Copernicus Berlin team may enter. This rule is important for safety, privacy, and insurance reasons. The accommodation is provided exclusively for scholarship holders, and we must ensure a secure and comfortable environment for everyone living there.
Can family members visit overnight?
Yes, your family members can visit you in Berlin. However, they cannot stay overnight in the Copernicus Berlin accommodation. Our housing is reserved exclusively for scholarship holders, so they will need to book their own accommodation in the city.
Is alcohol allowed?
No. During the IES programme, drinking alcohol alone or in small groups in Copernicus Berlin apartments or at events is not allowed. Exceptions are only possible for official group celebrations when supervisors are present. We strongly encourage a healthy, sport-oriented lifestyle. The programme is intensive and requires energy, focus, and productivity, so we expect scholarship holders to maintain habits that support their wellbeing.
Is smoking allowed?
No. Smoking is strictly prohibited inside the apartment, in the building, and even on the balcony. This rule is in place for safety, hygiene, and the comfort of all scholarship holders. All types of cigarettes and smoking products are not allowed: this includes regular cigarettes, e-cigarettes, vapes, IQOS and similar heated tobacco products, shisha, and any other smoking devices. Smoking is only allowed outside on the street in designated areas.
Is the apartment fully furnished?
Yes. The apartment is fully furnished and includes everything you need for comfortable living. You do not need to buy any additional furniture, although you are free to bring small items or decorations if you would like to make your space more personal.
Do I need to buy bedding?
No. We provide towels and bedsheets, which you can use and wash throughout the semester. If you prefer your own bedding or want to replace something, you are free to buy additional items yourself.
Does the apartment have a washing machine?
There is no individual washing machine in the apartment, but there is a laundry room available for all scholarship holders. The cost is 3.5 euro per wash and 1.5 euro per drying cycle. Detergent is not included, so you will need to buy it separately.
Do I need to clean the apartment myself?
Yes. There are no cleaning services provided, so you are responsible for keeping your room and the shared spaces clean. The apartments are equipped with everything you need for basic cleaning, so you can easily maintain the space yourself.
How often will the apartment be inspected?
There is no fixed schedule, but sometimes supervisors may check the apartments to ensure cleanliness and that everything is in good condition. These checks can happen without prior notification. The purpose is simply to make sure the living environment stays safe, comfortable, and well-maintained for everyone.
Where is the Copernicus Berlin Campus located?
Copernicus Berlin Campus is located right in the centre of Berlin, at Melchiorstr. 28. All Copernicus Berlin apartments are in this building, so you will be living in the heart of the city, with everything you need, as well as many sights and attractions, within walking distance.
Is the neighborhood safe?
Yes, the neighborhood is very safe. It is a lively and central area with plenty of students and young people around. Fun fact: we even have foxes, hedgehogs and squirrels living nearby, so the area is calm enough for wildlife to feel at home.
Are there quiet hours?
Yes. From 22:00 all scholarship holders are expected to be in their apartments and keep quiet. This helps ensure a comfortable living environment for everyone and respects the shared nature of the accommodation.
Is the accommodation gender-separated?
Mostly yes, but not always fully. Rooms are never mixed, so boys and girls are not placed in the same room. However, apartments themselves can sometimes be shared by scholarship holders of different genders, with each gender staying in their separate room.
What does the daily routine look like?
The daily routine of a scholarship holder is quite flexible and depends on your university schedule, volunteering tasks, and personal preferences. Typically it looks like this: Morning: university classes, personal studying, or volunteering tasks Afternoon: more classes, meetings with your volunteering supervisor, or work on assigned tasks Evening: free time, sports, exploring Berlin, or spending time with other scholarship holders Weekends: mostly free unless there is a planned activity or event During events the routine becomes more structured, and your attendance is required. Outside of these periods you have a lot of freedom to organize your day in a comfortable and productive way.
Will I meet previous scholarship holders?
Sometimes yes. For exceptional students and highly motivated volunteers we may extend the scholarship, so some members of our current team were scholarship holders in the past. You may meet them during meetings, events, and trainings, and they are always happy to share their experience and support new participants.
Are study absences covered in case of obligatory events?
If needed, we can provide an official letter, but in most cases you will need to manage absences directly with your professors. Each university and teacher has their own rules, so it is your responsibility to communicate with them in advance and make sure any missed classes are handled properly.
How does university enrollment work?
After you arrive in Berlin, Copernicus Berlin will guide you through the university enrollment process. You will receive detailed instructions, a list of required documents, and support from our team if you need help with the steps. However, the enrollment itself is your responsibility – you will need to visit the university, submit your documents, activate your student account, and complete all formalities. Each university may have slightly different procedures, but we will make sure you know exactly what to do and where to go.
How do I choose my courses?
You choose your courses by preparing a Learning Agreement, where you list the subjects you want to take at the university. You can select courses taught in English or German, depending on your language level and interests. The Learning Agreement is then reviewed and approved by your home university. This document defines your study plan for the semester and ensures that your chosen courses are recognized after you complete the program.
How many courses can I take?
You can take up to 3 courses. Managing more than that is usually very difficult, especially with exams, university deadlines, and adapting to life in Berlin.
Is it possible to work during the IES programme?
No, you cannot have a full-time job, neither in Germany nor in your home country, while you are a scholarship holder. However, you may do limited freelance work if you inform Copernicus Berlin in advance and it does not interfere with your studies or programme obligations.
How important are grades during the IES programme?
We encourage you to aim for a successful semester with good grades, but we understand that studying in a new environment and in another language can be challenging. More important than perfect grades are your motivation, active participation, and engagement in all parts of the IES programme.
How is the IES program funded?
The IES programme is funded by the own funds of Copernicus Berlin.
What expenses does the scholarship cover?
It depends on your scholarship type and status. Full conditions can be found in the description of each scholarship category.
When is the scholarship paid?
Pocket money is paid at the end of each month. During your first month in Berlin you will have enough time to complete all necessary German bureaucracy steps, including opening a bank account. Payments are transferred directly to your bank card and are not issued in cash.
Can I receive additional grants?
No. During the scholarship period you cannot receive additional grants or financial support from Copernicus Berlin beyond the benefits included in your scholarship type.
What is the selection deposit?
The selection deposit is a payment of 400 EUR required after you have been selected for the scholarship. Because the IES programme is highly competitive and receives thousands of applications for a limited number of spots, this deposit confirms that you are committed to coming to Berlin and participating in the program. Once you arrive in Berlin, the selection deposit is automatically converted into a safety deposit for your accommodation and professional behaviour. It serves as security in case of possible damages or serious inappropriate behaviour. The deposit is refunded only after the successful completion of the scholarship. Please note: The selection deposit is not refunded if you decide not to come to Berlin. If an emergency prevents you from coming, the deposit is still not refundable. If inconsistencies appear in your application or if performance during the preparation period is unsatisfactory, Copernicus Berlin reserves the right to cancel your participation without returning the deposit. The deposit ensures commitment, helps us plan the program in advance, and protects the accommodation fund for all scholarship holders.
What happens if I cause damage in the apartment?
If you cause any damage in the apartment, you are responsible for repairing or replacing the damaged item. If the damage is not properly fixed or paid for, the costs will be deducted from your safety deposit. In serious cases, additional costs may be charged if the damage exceeds the deposit amount.
What happens if I break a rule?
If a rule is broken, we always start with a conversation. We will talk with you, try to understand what happened, and find a reasonable solution together. Everyone can make a mistake, and our goal is to support you to improve and grow. However, if serious rules are violated or if smaller violations happen repeatedly despite warnings, Copernicus Berlin has the right to remove you from the program. In such cases the scholarship may be terminated and the deposit will not be refunded. We expect all scholarship holders to respect the rules, the accommodation, and the community.
Can I propose a topic for an event or workshop? Is such initiative welcome?
Yes, you can. Initiative is welcome and appreciated, and if your idea fits the values of Copernicus Berlin, you can always suggest it.
How far in advance are events approved and planning begun?
Usually one to three months in advance, and big events are planned four to six months before.
What types of events, besides training courses, does Copernicus Berlin organize?
Copernicus Berlin organizes a lot of different activities throughout the year, not only formal trainings. These include study visits to partner organizations, ideathons and small hackathons, online skill-building workshops, cultural evenings, community meetups, volunteering actions, project dissemination events, and informal networking gatherings. Overall, we organize a lot of events and always try to create an active, dynamic environment for our scholarship holders and partners.
How often are major events and training courses held?
Some periods have up to four training courses or major events in a single month, and other months may be lighter depending on project timelines. Overall, there is always a lot of fun and meaningful activity happening, and scholarship holders usually have many opportunities to join different events.
How are scholarship holders selected to attend specific event? Can I choose myself where I want to go?
Usually we send people to events where we need their support, but we also look carefully at your competencies, interests, language skills, and preferences. You can always tell us where you would like to go, and we do take it into account. Still, the final placement is done by the team based on event needs and overall balance.
How can I suggest improvements, ideas, and provide feedback?
You can write directly to the team, mention your ideas during calls, or use any feedback forms we share. Any format is completely fine. For scholarship holders we are always online, always nearby, and ready to discuss everything – suggestions, improvements, concerns, or new ideas.
Are there any specific cultural norms or etiquette within the Copernicus community that I should be aware of?
Be respectful, communicate clearly, keep deadlines, stay proactive, and ask questions when something is unclear.
How do you evaluate the engagement and performance of scholarship holders?
Engagement and performance are evaluated using a development scale at the end of every week during the IES programme. Together with your supervisors, you regularly reflect on your progress, participation, responsibilities, and behaviour in order to track your development and address any issues early.
Will I be able to see my own performance rating or evaluation results?
Yes. At the end of every week, you can see your results on the development scale. This allows you to follow your own progress, understand your strengths, and see where you can still improve during the IES programme.
Can I choose who I live with and in which specific apartment?
You can express preferences, but it depends on availability and cannot be guaranteed.
Will I have a temporary registration in Berlin?
Yes, you will receive the necessary documents from us, but you will need to arrange the registration (Anmeldung) yourself. It is simple, free, and required for things like banking, insurance, or phone contracts.
What are the monthly living costs in Berlin, and what is the average cost of groceries?
Berlin is not the cheapest city, but it is manageable for students. Most scholarship holders spend around 150 to 250 euros per month on groceries, depending on their cooking habits and lifestyle. Other living costs depend on the person, but overall it is similar to many big European cities, sometimes a bit more and sometimes a bit less.
Will you help with visa support and provide invitation letters?
Yes, Copernicus Berlin provides invitation letters and full support from our side during the visa process. We guide you through the steps and prepare the documents that must come from the organization. At the same time, you will need to collect a number of personal documents yourself, because embassies always require things like bank statements, university confirmations, insurance papers, and other personal proofs. We help with everything we can, and the rest is your part of the process.
How many applicants are selected each semester?
The number of selected applicants varies each semester and depends on the available funds and capacities of Copernicus Berlin. Therefore, we cannot guarantee a fixed number of scholarships for every semester.
Can I choose the university where I will do my exchange semester?
You can tell us your preferences for universities, and we will definitely take them into account. However, the final decision depends on where your profile fits best and where places are available. Our goal is to match you with a university that suits your academic background and the capacities of our partner institutions.
Who are the supervisors and what is their role?
Supervisors are part of the Copernicus Berlin team, and most of them were scholarship holders themselves in the past. They use their experience to guide and support you during the IES programme – in your daily life, studies, personal development, and integration in Berlin. They are your first contact persons for questions, challenges, and feedback.
Do I receive a certificate for completing the IES programme?
Yes. After successfully completing the IES programme, you will receive an official certificate from Copernicus Berlin. In addition, if you show outstanding performance and engagement, you may also receive a personalised recommendation letter, which can support your future academic and career opportunities.
Does IES help me with future opportunities (internships, master’s, jobs)?
Yes. Through the experience, skills, and network you gain during the IES programme, you have great opportunities to grow – both in your home country and in Germany. The programme can support your path towards internships, master’s studies, and future career steps by connecting you with professionals, organisations, and universities.

documents.title: Summer LF Program and Application Information
documents.text:
Leaders for future
Summer Internship Program
Summer joy and professional growth
Summer Internship Program LEADERS for FUTURE takes place once a year in summer (from July 1 to August 31). The minimum duration of the program is two months. The application period is approximately 9 months.
Summer Internship Program LEADERS for FUTURE combines summer fun with professional development. The program runs from July 01 to August 31.
There is also the option to participate in the program for up to 6 months. If you wish to participate in the program for more than 2 months, there will be a fee (980 EUR) for each additional month, regardless of the type of scholarship awarded.
Berlin, as one of Europe's largest cities, offers broad development opportunities across many career fields. After a successful application process, participants receive practical guidance and support before starting their internship journey.
Summer LF is
Travel
Spend your summer in Berlin and discover Germany while also having access to nearby European destinations during your stay.
Language
Improve both your German and English in a practical international environment and through organized German courses.
Networking
Build strong friendships and professional connections with participants from different countries and backgrounds.
Experience
Gain practical internship experience in Germany while strengthening your professional, social, and intercultural skills.
Requirements
Applicants must be at least 18 years old at the time of application.
Applicants from all countries are welcome.
All occupations are welcome.
Good knowledge of English or German (at least B1).
Be motivated, independent and responsible.
Accommodation
Program participants will be accommodated in the comfortable and furnished apartments of the Copernicus Berlin Campus (CBC). The apartments are centrally located and consist of single and double rooms. High-speed internet and cable television are also available. In addition, there is a laundry service on campus.
CBC is located in the center of Berlin. Nearby are the sights of Berlin: Alexanderplatz, Berlin Cathedral, Museum Island, East Side Gallery, Warschauer Strasse, Oberbaumbruecke and more. Numerous parks and sports facilities make life on campus pleasant and healthy. In the surrounding area you can also find large shopping malls, supermarkets, organic food stores, bakeries, pharmacies, hospitals, gyms, tennis courts, swimming pools and much more.
Copernicus Berlin Campus is also well connected by public transportation. There are several buses, subways and suburban trains nearby. So you can reach any place in Berlin in very short time.
Program fees
The costs for accommodation, liability insurance, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN.
Travel expenses are not covered.
Funding opportunities
Program participants can apply for full, partial scholarships or participate at their own costs. On the application form you have the possibility to choose accordingly.
Full Scholarship
High competition
Pocket money is paid monthly;
The costs for accommodation, liability insurance, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN;
Travel expenses are not covered.
Partial Scholarship
Medium competition
Pocket money is paid monthly;
The costs for liability insurance, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN;
Travel expenses are not covered.
Participant needs to cover the cost for accommodation at the Copernicus Berlin Campus completely themselves.
Own Cost
Low competition
Participant needs to cover all the costs completely themselves.
A full scholarship of the Leaders for Future program for one person consists of the following parts
Accommodation - 5880 EUR
Administrative expenses - 650 EUR
Consultancy and support - 250 EUR
Events - 590 EUR
Liability insurance - 100 EUR
Pocket money - 400 EUR
Application documents
For the successful application is required a complete chronological curriculum vitae. It should be written chronologically and in German or English.
Complete chronological curriculum vitae (CV)
with photo.
It should be written chronologically and in German or in English. It is important to mention following categories in your CV: academical background, working or volunteering background.
To create good CV you can use our CV-Builder.
Application procedure
The application procedure consists of two stages:
Online application via the application form
Application period: September 1, 2024 - October 10, 2024
›
Interview phase
After completing the tasks, selected candidates may be invited for a personal interview.
Apply Now
FAQ
Where am I going to live?
During the LEADERS for FUTURE Program, participants are accommodated in a comfortable and furnished apartment at the Copernicus Berlin Campus in the heart of Berlin.
Who will I share the apartment with?
The apartment consists of 3 rooms: Single bedroom, two-bed room and living room. There are only three participants in the apartment. Each participant has his or her own bed, desk, closet and private internet connection. Kitchen and bathroom are shared by the three participants.
Is food provided during the scholarship program?
No. Participants should take care of their own food.
Should I pay for utility services?
No. The costs for water, hot water, heating, internet and cable TV are paid directly by Copernicus Berlin.
Where will I do my internship?
The internship is an important part of the LF program and is designed to provide international work experience and to develop personal and professional skills. The COPERNICUS BERLIN team will support the LF participants with advice and assistance. There will be also offered internships at COPERNICUS BERLIN.
Do I have to be vaccinated to attend the program?
No.
Are there any Covid restrictions for travel into Germany?
Mostly no. Germany allows entry to travelers from most countries, regardless of their Covid-19 vaccination status but it is important to check that this information applies to you and to the country you are travelling from. More information you can find here: https://www.bundesgesundheitsministerium.de/en/service/gesetze-und-verordnungen/guv-19-lp/coronavirus-einreiseverordnung.html
Is Berlin safe?
YES. Berlin is considered a safe destination for international travelers but as with many other major cities, pickpocketing is somewhat common in the busy city center and crowded tourist attractions. For your safety, it is best to always remain alert and be aware of your surroundings.
What can you expect?
Unforgettable time in Germany; Improvement of German and English language skills; Personal and professional development; Intercultural communication; Networking and making friends; Learning to think critically and analytically.
What is the cancellation policy?
We understand that various situations may occur which prevent participation. However, due to the limited number of spots and high demand for the Leaders for Future program, every reserved place means that another applicant could not be admitted. Therefore, the following cancellation rules apply in all cases - whether due to personal reasons, unforeseen circumstances, visa issues, or a personal decision not to participate: Cancellation 4 months or more before the official program start date: 80 percent of the paid amount will be refunded. Cancellation less than 4 months before the official program start date: Only 20 percent of the paid amount will be refunded. Cancellation after the program has started: No refunds will be provided.

documents.title: Professionals in Residence Program
documents.text:
Scholarships
Professional in Residence (PiR) Scholarship
Advanced alumni track for former IES scholarship holders focused on leadership, mentorship, and practical impact.
Advanced alumni track for former IES scholarship holders.
The Professional in Residence (PiR) Scholarship is Copernicus Berlin's premium alumni track for former International Excellence Scholarship (IES) participants who are ready to move from participant to community builder. PiR is designed for people who already know the Copernicus Berlin culture, values, and standards and who now want to take the next step: lead, mentor, create impact, and become a trusted reference point for the next cohort. PiR gives you a structured framework to grow into a role with real responsibility: you become a supervisor and mentor for new scholarship holders, while simultaneously contributing to Copernicus Berlin projects in your own specialization (IT, media, design, training, project development, communications, research, operations, and more). PiR is a scholarship-based program built around learning-by-doing, leadership practice, and tangible outputs that matter.
What makes PiR special?
You don't just join, you shape.
PiR participants actively shape the scholarship community. You help create structure, clarity, and stability especially for newcomers who arrive in Berlin with excitement, but also many questions.
Real responsibility, not symbolic titles
As a PiR, you are trusted with responsibilities that influence the quality and success of the IES experience. Your role is meaningful: you support onboarding, ensure smooth integration, and strengthen the culture of respect, discipline, and collaboration.
Your specialization becomes impact.
PiR is also a chance to develop real project outputs aligned with your profile whether you build digital tools, produce media content, design systems, facilitate learning, develop partnerships, or contribute to grant writing and project implementation.
A portfolio you can show
Your PiR experience is not just participation. It creates a visible track record: deliverables, teamwork, leadership, and a reliable contribution to a respected NGO ecosystem in Berlin.
A pathway to long-term engagement
PiR is the bridge between the IES scholarship experience and long-term professional involvement in international projects, civic innovation, and European cooperation.
Funding opportunities
Full Scholarship
PiR participants can receive a full scholarship package built to support practical contribution, mentoring, and professional growth in Berlin.
*The base pocket money is EUR 500/month.
In certain cases, the amount can be increased when a PiR participant regularly brings additional resources to Copernicus Berlin for example through successful grants, fundraising, sponsorships, or sustained unpaid contributions across multiple projects beyond the standard PiR role.
Pocket money is paid monthly.
Health insurance costs are reimbursed.
Accommodation, enrollment, liability insurance, educational trips, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring, and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN.
Travel expenses are not covered.
Application documents
The following document is required for a successful application:
Complete chronological curriculum vitae (CV)
with photo.
It should be written chronologically and in German or in English. It is important to mention following categories in your CV: academical background, working or volunteering background.
To create good CV you can use our CV-Builder.
*Please note that a language
certificate for German or English
is
not required
for the application. If the application is complete and convincing, the selection committee will assess the language skills accordingly during the interview.
**We also
do not require
a
university transcript
because we believe that your potential and motivation go far beyond grades.
Eligibility
PiR is open
exclusively
to candidates who
Previously participated in the IES Scholarship for at least 6 month
Successfully completed the program with strong reliability and community contribution
Are ready to support new scholarship holders through structured supervision and mentoring, and
Can commit to an active role in Copernicus Berlin projects aligned with their specialization.
A full scholarship of the IES program for one person amounts to Approx. 15,000 EUR and consists of the following parts
Accommondation - 5400 EUR
Administrative costs - 790 EUR
Consulting and mentoring - 750 EUR
Cultural events - 250 EUR
Educational tour - 560 EUR
Enrollment fee - 320 EUR
Health Insurance - 900 EUR
International seminars - 1330 EUR
Liability insurance - 110 EUR
Pocket money - 3000 EUR
Social meetings - 700 EUR
Welcome pack and materials - 350 EUR
Workshops and trainings - 540 EUR
Apply Now

documents.title: Ukrainian Talent Scholarship in Berlin
documents.text:
Scholarships
A scholarship in Berlin for ambitious Ukrainians who want to study, grow professionally, and become part of an international community.
The Ukrainian Talent Scholarship is designed for talented young people from Ukraine who are ready to invest in their future. The programme combines academic development, practical experience, mentoring, and community engagement in Berlin.
What is the Ukrainian Talent Scholarship?
The Ukrainian Talent Scholarship is a Berlin-based support programme for motivated Ukrainians who want to combine learning, practical engagement, and personal development in an international setting.
The programme offers a unique combination of academic opportunities, project-based experience, mentoring, workshops, and community life. Participants are supported not only in their educational journey, but also in building confidence, networks, and practical competencies for the future.
It is created for those who want to take initiative, develop their talents, and use their potential in a meaningful way.
What you gain?
Academic development
Strengthen your knowledge and expand your international perspective through study-related learning and educational opportunities in Berlin.
Practical experience
Take part in real projects and gain hands-on experience in areas such as youth work, media, communication, project management, IT, design, events, or community engagement.
Mentoring and guidance
Receive support through onboarding, mentoring, and structured guidance throughout your journey.
International environment
Join a diverse and inspiring community, build valuable connections, and experience life in one of Europe’s most vibrant cities.
Personal growth
Develop confidence, responsibility, communication skills, and the ability to work in multicultural and professional contexts.
Who should apply
The Ukrainian Talent Scholarship is for ambitious and motivated Ukrainians who want to develop academically, professionally, and personally in an international environment.
We are looking for applicants who are open-minded, proactive, and ready to contribute actively to programme life.
Students at Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD level
Recent graduates and young talents
Motivated individuals with strong learning interest
Applicants with initiative, responsibility, and commitment
Ukrainians who want to gain experience in Berlin and build their future in a European context
Why Berlin
Berlin offers a unique environment for learning, creativity, and international exchange. As a city known for innovation, diversity, and cultural openness, it provides an inspiring setting for academic growth and professional development.
For scholarship holders, Berlin becomes more than a destination. It becomes a place to build experience, confidence, networks, and new perspectives.
Scholarship types
Full Scholarship
Pocket money is paid monthly;
Health insurance costs are reimbursed;
The costs for accommodation, enrollment, liability insurance, educational trips, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN;
Travel expenses are not covered.
Partial Scholarship Plus
Pocket money is paid monthly;
The costs for accommodation, enrollment, liability insurance, educational trips, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN;
Travel expenses are not covered;
Health insurance costs are not covered.
Partial Scholarship
Pocket money is paid monthly;
The costs for enrollment, liability insurance, educational trips, social meetings, administration, welcome package, cultural events, international seminars, consulting, mentoring and workshops are covered directly by COPERNICUS BERLIN;
Travel expenses are not covered;
Health insurance costs are not covered;
Accommodation costs for two months (1.650 EUR) have to be paid, the rest will be covered by COPERNICUS BERLIN.
Community and contribution
Participants are not only recipients of support. They are also active members of a wider community. Through involvement in projects, team activities, and shared experiences, scholarship holders contribute to an environment based on learning, collaboration, and positive impact.
This creates a stronger sense of belonging, responsibility, and real participation.
Application documents
The following document is required for a successful application:
Complete chronological curriculum vitae (CV)
with photo.
It should be written chronologically and in German or in English. It is important to mention following categories in your CV: academical background, working/volunteering background.
To create good CV you can use our CV-Builder.
*Please note that a language
certificate for German or English is not required
for the application. If the application is complete and convincing, the selection committee will assess the language skills accordingly during the interview.
**We also
do not require a university transcript
, because we believe that your potential and motivation go far beyond grades. However, the hosting university may request academic documents at a later stage.
Application process
Step 1
Submit your application
Complete the online application form and provide the required documents.
›
Step 2
Application review
Each application is reviewed carefully based on motivation, profile, and programme fit.
›
Step 3
Selection process
Shortlisted applicants may be invited for the next steps of the selection process.
›
Step 4
Start your journey
Selected participants receive onboarding information and begin their scholarship experience in Berlin.
Apply Now
